name: Die Hard
description: John McClane, Nakatomi Plaza, and the 1988 action film that defined the genre.

questions.q: Who stars as New York cop John McClane in Die Hard (1988)?

source.notes: Willis was best known for Moonlighting at the time.

questions.q: What is the name of the Los Angeles skyscraper where most of Die Hard takes place?

source.notes: In real life: Fox Plaza in Century City.

questions.q: Which actor plays cool-as-ice main villain Hans Gruber?

source.notes: It was Rickman's first feature-film role.

questions.q: What holiday is Die Hard set during — the reason fans argue it's a Christmas movie?

source.notes: McClane flies to LA for a Christmas Eve office party.

questions.q: What is the name of McClane's estranged wife, whose office party he's attending?

source.notes: Holly Gennaro-McClane.

questions.q: Die Hard's memorable shirt color for the barefoot McClane is what?

source.notes: He sheds his dress shirt early on.

questions.q: Die Hard's iconic catchphrase from McClane is what?
choices: Say hello to my little friend

source.notes: Repeated in every Die Hard sequel.

questions.q: Which actor plays Sgt. Al Powell, McClane's reassuring LAPD radio contact?

source.notes: VelJohnson later starred on Family Matters.

questions.q: Who directed Die Hard?

source.notes: McTiernan also directed Predator and The Hunt for Red October.

questions.q: Die Hard is based on which 1979 novel?

source.original_a: Nothing Lasts Forever by Roderick Thorp
source.notes: By Roderick Thorp — itself a sequel to 1966's The Detective.

questions.q: How many hostages — approximately — are taken in the Nakatomi Plaza heist?

source.notes: About 30 Nakatomi Corporation employees.

questions.q: What is Hans Gruber's real goal in seizing Nakatomi Plaza?

source.notes: Gruber is a thief disguised as a terrorist.

questions.q: Who plays the smarmy TV reporter Richard Thornburg?

source.notes: Atherton specialized in weaselly 80s authority figures.

questions.q: Who composed the suspenseful Die Hard score, quoting Ode to Joy throughout?

source.notes: Kamen also scored Lethal Weapon.

questions.q: Die Hard's cinematographer, who later became a blockbuster director himself (Speed, Twister), is who?

source.notes: De Bont directed Speed (1994) and Twister (1996).

questions.q: Which Austrian ballet dancer plays Gruber's ponytailed henchman Karl?

source.notes: Godunov was a star dancer of the Bolshoi Ballet.

questions.q: Which A-list action star was offered the role of John McClane but turned it down to make Twins?

source.notes: Schwarzenegger did Twins (1988) with Danny DeVito instead.

questions.q: How much did Bruce Willis reportedly earn for starring in Die Hard?

source.notes: Unusually large for a relatively unknown film lead at the time.

questions.q: Due to a 1968 contract from his earlier film 'The Detective,' Fox was legally obligated to offer John McClane to which star first?

source.notes: Sinatra, then 73, declined.

questions.q: The fictional 'Nakatomi Plaza' is — in real life — the headquarters of which Hollywood studio?

source.notes: Fox also distributed Die Hard.

questions.q: Which action star plays Simon Gruber, Hans's brother, in 1995's Die Hard with a Vengeance?

source.notes: Irons's Simon is Hans Gruber's avenging brother.

questions.q: How many films are in the Die Hard franchise, as of 2013's A Good Day to Die Hard?
